features.description: Fix critical layout bug: LegalDisclaimer footer renders as a full-height, full-width banner at the TOP of the page instead of at the bottom. Root cause: (1) Footer is a direct flex item of `.app-layout` alongside nav and main content, causing it to be laid out in the flex row. (2) Footer has `width: 100%` making it span full layout width. (3) Flex `align-items: stretch` (default) makes it stretch to full layout height (1280x887px). Solution: Restructure Layout.tsx so footer is OUTSIDE the flex row. Wrap nav + main in a `.app-layout__content` flex row, place footer as a block element below. Update Layout.css: `.app-layout { flex-direction: column; min-height: 100vh }`, `.app-layout__content { display: flex; flex: 1 }`, nav and main as flex items in the content row. Footer becomes a block below the content flex.
preconditions: Frontend dev server running on port 3101
expectedBehavior: Footer renders BELOW all content, not as a banner at the top
Footer has natural content height (~50-60px), not full viewport height
Footer does NOT span full viewport width - only its container width
Footer visible at bottom of page on all pages and breakpoints
verificationSteps: Manual: Set viewport to 1280x800, take full-page screenshot - footer at bottom, not spanning full height
Verify footer's computed styles: height should be ~50-60px, not 887px
npm run typecheck && npm run lint

features.description: Fix critical layout bug: Main content area collapses to ~64px wide on desktop instead of filling available space. Root cause: After fixing the footer layout (moving it out of the flex row), the main content flex item `.app-layout__main` should properly expand to fill available space between sidebar and page edge. The `.app-layout__main` should have `flex: 1; min-width: 0` to fill available space. Inner content (home-page, etc.) should use `max-width` with `margin: 0 auto` for centering. Ensure the welcome card uses appropriate width (e.g., 100% up to max-width) so it fills the main area on desktop.
preconditions: Frontend dev server running on port 3101
fix-layout-structure-footer-banner must be done first
expectedBehavior: Main content area fills available horizontal space on desktop (1280px viewport - 240px sidebar = ~1040px available)
Welcome card centered within main area with comfortable margins
Content does NOT collapse to narrow column
Layout feels balanced and professional on desktop
verificationSteps: Manual: Set viewport to 1280x800, measure main content width - should be >900px
Screenshot at 1280px showing properly sized content area
npm run typecheck && npm run lint

features.description: Fix session page content. After fixing the layout structure, verify the meditation chat interface renders correctly. The `/session` page should show the MeditationChat component with Marcus icon, 'Meditation with Marcus Aurelius' heading, description text, and 'Begin Meditation' button. The meditation nav item should be highlighted as active. No home page welcome card elements should appear.
preconditions: Frontend dev server running on port 3101
fix-layout-structure-footer-banner must be done first
expectedBehavior: Session page shows meditation chat welcome UI (Marcus icon, heading, Begin button)
Session page does NOT show home page welcome card
Meditation nav item is highlighted as active
No duplicate home page elements
verificationSteps: Manual: Navigate to /session at 1280px, verify meditation chat welcome UI renders
npm run typecheck && npm run lint

features.description: Fix mobile bottom navigation floating over content. The nav has proper CSS (`position: fixed; bottom: 0`) but content may render behind it. Need to ensure: (1) nav has `position: fixed; bottom: 0; z-index` properly set, (2) main content area has `padding-bottom` equal to nav height so content doesn't get hidden, (3) no overflow content behind the nav.
preconditions: Frontend dev server running on port 3101
expectedBehavior: At 375px mobile viewport, bottom nav is fixed to viewport bottom
Content scrolls above the nav without being covered
Last line of content is visible above the nav
verificationSteps: Manual: Set viewport to iPhone 12 (375px), navigate to home page, scroll to bottom - nav at viewport bottom, content not covered
Take annotated screenshot showing nav at bottom, last content visible

expectedBehavior: Home page: Home nav item active only
Session page: Meditation nav item active only
History page: History nav item active only
Profile page: Profile nav item active only
Settings page: Settings nav item active only

features.description: Fix desktop navigation showing labels. At 1280px desktop viewport, nav items should show BOTH icons AND text labels (Home, Meditation, History, Profile, Settings). Labels are present in DOM but may be hidden by CSS. Check the desktop breakpoint CSS (1200px+) that controls label visibility. Ensure `.navigation__link-label` is visible at desktop widths. The nav should be 240px wide at desktop with full labels.

expectedBehavior: At 1280px, nav shows icons + text labels: Home, Meditation, History, Profile, Settings
Labels are readable and not truncated
Nav is 240px wide at desktop

Screenshot showing nav with icons AND text labels

features.description: Resolve CSS conflicts between App.css and HomePage.css. Both files define .home-page, .home-header, .welcome-card, .disclaimer, .tagline. App.css imports AFTER HomePage.css causing unpredictable cascade. Solution: Remove duplicate class definitions from App.css, keeping only global app-level styles. HomePage.css contains all .home-page related styles. HomePage.tsx imports only HomePage.css for page styles.
preconditions: Frontend dev server running on port 3101
expectedBehavior: App.css contains only global .app styles, not page-specific duplicates
HomePage.css contains all .home-page related styles
No CSS class conflicts or duplicate definitions
verificationSteps: Search for duplicate class definitions across all CSS files
Verify home page renders identically after fix
npm run typecheck

features.description: Remove duplicate branding and legal disclaimers from home page. Currently: OpenMarcus appears 4x (sidebar logo, home header h1, card h2, brand text) and disclaimer appears 3x (header banner, welcome card note, footer). Fix: (1) Remove home-header from HomePage.tsx - sidebar already has branding, (2) Keep disclaimer in welcome card OR footer, not both, (3) The welcome card's 'Welcome to OpenMarcus' h2 is fine as page section heading.
preconditions: Frontend dev server running on port 3101
expectedBehavior: Home page shows: sidebar branding (once) + welcome card with greeting + 'Begin Meditation'
Legal disclaimer appears at most twice (footer + optional card note)
No duplicate header/brand section above the welcome card
verificationSteps: Manual: Visit home page at 1280px, count branding occurrences (max 3: sidebar logo+text, card heading)
Count disclaimer occurrences (max 2)
Screenshot showing clean layout

features.description: Fix card-within-card nesting. ProfileDisplay renders inside welcome-card with its own border/shadow, creating nested borders. Fix: Move ProfileDisplay styling to work WITHIN the welcome card (remove its own border/shadow), OR restructure HomePage so ProfileDisplay is NOT inside welcome-card div, OR style ProfileDisplay as inline content within the card.
preconditions: Frontend dev server running on port 3101
expectedBehavior: Welcome card is one cohesive card with rounded corners, no visible nested borders
ProfileDisplay content blends naturally into the welcome card
No double-border or box-in-box appearance
verificationSteps: Manual: Visit home page at 1280px, inspect welcome card for nested borders
Screenshot showing clean single-card design

Primary gets primary color; secondary gets secondary color. Both clearly distinguishable.

expectedBehavior: All primary buttons use consistent primary style
All secondary buttons use consistent secondary style
Contrast ratios meet WCAG AA standards

Both follow same button design system
Screenshot for before/after

features.description: Fix UX confusion: clicking 'Begin Meditation' on home page navigates to /session where user must click 'Begin Meditation' AGAIN. This double-click is confusing. Solution options: (a) Make the home page 'Begin Meditation' button START the meditation session directly (navigate to /session AND call beginSession), OR (b) Remove the 'Begin Meditation' button from the session page's idle state and auto-start, OR (c) Make the home page button link directly to the active session view. Recommended: Option (a) - keep both buttons but the home page button starts the session so going to /session shows the active chat, not another begin prompt.
preconditions: Frontend dev server running on port 3101
expectedBehavior: Clicking 'Begin Meditation' on home page starts the meditation session
User goes directly to active chat with Marcus greeting
No need to click 'Begin Meditation' twice
verificationSteps: Manual: Click 'Begin Meditation' on home page, verify session starts immediately
Screenshot showing active chat, not another begin prompt

features.description: Fix voice input error message to be actionable. When STT server is not running, the error shown should clearly state: what went wrong AND how to fix it. Current message may be generic. New message should include: 'STT server is not running. To start it: cd servers/stt && node server.mjs --port 8765'
preconditions: Frontend dev server running on port 3101
expectedBehavior: When STT server is down, error message clearly states the problem
Error message includes the command to start the server
User can fix the issue without external documentation
verificationSteps: Manual: Ensure STT server is NOT running, click mic button, verify error message
Screenshot showing actionable error message with start command

features.description: Fix voice output icon states to be visually clear. The speaker button must have clearly distinguishable states: (1) DISABLED: muted speaker icon, grey/outlined style. (2) ENABLED (idle): full speaker icon, colored/highlighted to show it's ON. (3) SPEAKING: animated speaker icon with sound waves, distinct from enabled idle. The enabled state should NOT look similar to the disabled state. The current icon may only differ by a subtle X change which is unclear.
preconditions: Frontend dev server running on port 3101
expectedBehavior: Disabled: grey muted speaker, clearly OFF
Enabled (idle): colored speaker, clearly ON
Speaking: animated speaker with waves, clearly ACTIVE
States are visually intuitive - user knows at a glance whether TTS is on or off
verificationSteps: Manual: Toggle voice output, observe icon changes. Take screenshots of each state.
Verify each state is visually distinct
